new_classes.label_en: Whiskies other than Scotch whisky, in containers holding > 2 l (CN 2208 30 79)
new_classes.label_de: Whisky (ausgen. Scotch Whisky), in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von mehr als 2 l (KN 2208 30 79)
new_classes.definition_en: Whisky distilled from a mash of cereals, marketed at an alcoholic strength by volume of 40% vol or higher, other than Scotch whisky (i.e. not distilled and matured in Scotland), put up in containers holding more than 2 litres. Whisky-soda (whisky with added aerated water) is excluded.

coverage_explanation: All principal distinguishing criteria from the legal text are captured: (1) whisky character — spirit distilled from a mash of cereals, modelled via producedBy some WhiskyGrainMashDistillationProcess and has_quality some WhiskyQuality; (2) NOT Scotch whisky — modelled as complement of ScotchWhiskyOriginQuality; (3) NOT whisky-soda — modelled as complement of SodaWaterAdditionQuality; (4) large container (> 2 L) — modelled via hasPart some LargeContainer; (5) alcoholic strength ≥ 40% vol — modelled as decimalRange minInclusive 40.0 on actualAlcoholicStrengthByVolumePercent. A minor gap (score reduced to 0.92): the legal note covers the full range 22083011–22083088 (all whiskies), and this subheading sits within the "other whiskies" branch (i.e. also excludes blended malt Scotch, single malt Scotch, etc.) but those sibling exclusions are implicit from the classification hierarchy rather than expressly stated in the snippet provided. The maturation requirement ("matured") is mentioned only for Scotch whisky and is not a positive discriminator for this subheading. The container threshold (> 2 L) uses the existing eucn:LargeContainer (≥ 2 L) class; CN 2208 30 79 uses strictly > 2 L, while LargeContainer is defined as ≥ 2 L — this is a marginal modelling approximation since the sibling small-container code uses < 2 L, making the 2 L boundary effectively exclusive on the large side.
